description: TCP kernel functions for network observability

answers: why is this service failing to connect to a backend
which connections are being refused
which connections are timing out
how long does it take to establish a connection
is a specific backend unreachable
probes.use_when: you need to know which connections are being attempted, which are failing, and what errno the kernel returned. fexit gives you the return value so you know immediately if the connect succeeded or failed.
probes.not_when: you only care about established connections — use tcp_close for lifetime, tcp_retransmit_skb for reliability.

interpretations.action: normal — correlate with tcp_close to get connection lifetime

interpretations.conclusion: nothing is listening on dst_ip:dst_port — process crashed, wrong port, or misconfigured
interpretations.action: check if the target process is running on dst_ip. check port configuration.

interpretations.conclusion: host exists but is not responding — overloaded, hung, or network path is broken
interpretations.action: check network path between src and dst. check if dst host is overloaded.

interpretations.conclusion: no route to host — routing problem or host is down
interpretations.action: check routing table. check if dst host is up.

interpretations.conclusion: network is unreachable — interface down or routing misconfiguration
interpretations.action: check network interfaces and routing configuration on src host.

interpretations.pattern: high rate of ret != 0 to same dst_ip:dst_port
interpretations.conclusion: persistent failure to a specific backend — not transient
interpretations.action: correlate with RAUTA HTTP errors on same 4-tuple in AHTI. this is a backend health issue.

answers: how long do connections to this backend last
which connections are being closed
which process owns a connection
is a backend cycling connections too quickly

fields.name: src_port
fields.meaning: source port — identifies the specific connection

fields.name: pid
fields.meaning: process ID that owned the connection

probes.use_when: you want to track connection lifetime or understand connection churn. combine with tcp_connect on the same 4-tuple to calculate duration.
probes.not_when: you need failure reasons — tcp_connect gives you errno. you need reliability signals — use tcp_retransmit_skb.

interpretations.pattern: tcp_close fires immediately after tcp_connect on same 4-tuple
interpretations.conclusion: connection was opened and immediately closed — likely a health check or failed handshake
interpretations.action: normal for health check patterns. investigate if unexpected.

interpretations.pattern: high rate of tcp_close to same dst_ip:dst_port
interpretations.conclusion: connection churn — connections being opened and closed rapidly
interpretations.action: check if connection pooling is misconfigured. high churn wastes TCP handshake overhead.

answers: is the network dropping packets
why is this connection slow
which backend has network problems
is this a network problem or an application problem
which specific connection is experiencing packet loss

fields.name: tcp_state
fields.meaning: TCP state when retransmit fired — critical for diagnosis

probes.use_when: you suspect network-layer problems. retransmits are the kernel's signal that packets are being lost. the tcp_state field tells you exactly what kind of problem it is.
probes.not_when: you want to know if a connection succeeded or failed — use tcp_connect. retransmits mean the connection exists but packets are being lost.

tcp_states.1: ESTABLISHED — connection is up, packets being lost mid-stream
tcp_states.2: SYN_SENT — initial handshake failing, remote may be unreachable
tcp_states.4: FIN_WAIT1 — connection teardown, usually not critical
tcp_states.7: CLOSE_WAIT — remote closed but local hasn't, application may be hung
tcp_states.8: LAST_ACK — final teardown, usually not critical

interpretations.conclusion: initial handshake is failing — remote is unreachable, firewall dropping SYN packets, or host is down
interpretations.action: check connectivity to dst_ip from this node. this is NOT an application problem — the connection never established.

interpretations.conclusion: packets are being lost on an active connection — network congestion, switch issue, or physical layer problem between nodes
interpretations.action: check network path between src and dst nodes. this is a network problem, not application. correlate with RAUTA HTTP latency spikes on same 4-tuple.

interpretations.conclusion: remote closed the connection but local application has not read/closed — possible application hang or slow consumer
interpretations.action: check application on src — it may be hung or not reading from the socket.

interpretations.pattern: 3+ retransmits in 10s on same 4-tuple, tcp_state == ESTABLISHED
interpretations.conclusion: persistent packet loss to a specific backend — network path is degraded
interpretations.action: this is the kernel telling you the network between these two IPs is broken. escalate to network team. not an application bug.

interpretations.pattern: retransmits on multiple 4-tuples to same dst_ip
interpretations.conclusion: all connections to this host are experiencing packet loss — host-level network issue
interpretations.action: the problem is at dst_ip or the network path to it, not a specific connection. check node network interface and switch port.

interpretations.pattern: retransmits on multiple 4-tuples to multiple dst_ips
interpretations.conclusion: widespread packet loss — local node network interface issue or upstream switch
interpretations.action: check local node network interface. check upstream switch. this affects all traffic from this node.

answers: how much data is a process sending
which process is a heavy network sender
is this connection saturated
probes.use_when: you need per-connection, per-process send throughput. high frequency — consider sampling (sample_rate: 0.1) for busy nodes.
probes.not_when: you just need to know if a connection exists — use tcp_connect. tcp_sendmsg fires on every send call, which can be thousands per second.

interpretations.pattern: high bytes per call, low retransmits
interpretations.action: normal. monitor for retransmits if latency increases.

interpretations.pattern: high bytes per call, high retransmits on same 4-tuple
interpretations.conclusion: connection is saturating and experiencing loss — possible congestion
interpretations.action: correlate with tcp_retransmit_skb. network path may be congested.

answers: how fast is a server accepting connections
is the accept backlog filling up
which process is accepting connections on a port
probes.use_when: you want to observe incoming connection acceptance from the server side. pairs with tcp_connect on the client side for full picture.
probes.not_when: you only care about outbound connections — use tcp_connect.

interpretations.conclusion: accept backlog is full — server cannot keep up with incoming connections
interpretations.action: increase listen backlog. check if server is overloaded. connections are being dropped.

interpretations.conclusion: server stopped accepting — process may be hung or overloaded
interpretations.action: check server process health. correlate with RAUTA HTTP latency.
